title: Docs convergence + hygiene strand. Makes the whole rosetta corpus tell the new truth (v1.5 stacks are content-self-contained: a per-stack Directus serving the captured public library on --local-content, prod-read fallback otherwise) by correcting the stale local-Directus claims (a fictional directus/directus:10.10.1 docker service with admin/password creds + a compose snippet — the platform compose has NO directus service, verified) and sweeping the print-only/exit-4/reads-live-from-prod framing across the skills + CLAUDE.md + the demo corpus. Absorbs the 4 aged-out hygiene items the deferral audit surfaced: (a) an explicit Go toolchain go1.25.11 pin (clears the 12 called-stdlib advisories, lazy), (b) a corpus README-index-row guard (a lint that fails when a new doc lacks its directory-README index row), (c) the alignment zero-critical-genes guard (reject the vacuous-100% DNA), (d) the /project-stats scope fix (stop scanning the gitignored stack-*/ platform clones).
position_note: M24 is the 4th milestone of v1.5 'prop room' (M21->M25). M21 made the directus replay exit 0 + serve anonymously; M22 boots + verifies the per-stack instance as a compose service; M23 points services at it + closes referential gaps; M24 tells the corpus-wide truth + absorbs the 4-item hygiene strand; M25 proves it live. Strictly sequential. After this close: M25 (the field-bake observable-behavior gate — the final milestone of v1.5).
kind: a mostly-docs section milestone. The rosetta m24/docs-hygiene branch carries the corpus-wide truth-up (3 doc sections: stale local-Directus corrections + known-state/safety/directus-local rewrites + the corpus-wide language sweep) plus the plan/doc records. The hygiene strand's executable code is split: 3 items land in the gitignored rosetta-extensions authoring copy (Go pin on 4 go.mod + clerkenstein CI; the alignment zero-critical guard in compare.go/dna.go; the NEW stack-core/corpus_index_guard.py README-index lint), and 1 item (§7 /project-stats scope fix) lands cross-repo in the developer-kit plugin's stats.sh (ant-singularity 825cdce) — where the script actually lives. ext tag prop-room-m24 set by the orchestrator post-close.

sections.list: §1 stale local-Directus corrections (rosetta 84cb8c0) · §2 known-state/safety/directus-local rewrites (rosetta 409b449) · §3 corpus-wide language sweep via /update-knowledge (rosetta b95ee07) · §4 (a) Go toolchain go1.25.11 pin (ext 423bac7) · §5 (b) README index-row guard + 7-gap backfill (ext d6dd8fc, fix 191d650; rosetta 52f68d8) · §6 (c) zero-critical-genes guard (ext 04de89e, harden d01a3ee) · §7 (d) /project-stats scope fix (developer-kit/ant-singularity 825cdce)

tests.language: Go (alignment — the M24-touched module: the zero-critical-genes guard) + python (stack-core — the NEW corpus_index_guard.py + its suite, stdlib unittest). The rosetta-side doc sections (§1-§3) carry no executable code. §4 (Go pin) is config-only (no test surface). §7 (/project-stats) is a small cross-repo shell script in the developer-kit plugin, left as-is per scope.
tests.go_delta_note: M24 added +6 Go test funcs, ALL in alignment (46->52): the zero-critical-genes guard — +3 in internal/dna/validate_test.go (Validate rejects zero-critical; criticalGenes counts VARIANTS not capabilities; Load->Validate end-to-end) + +3 in internal/compare/compare_test.go (GateMet refuses a non-zero critical gate at 0 critical genes; CriticalGenes count; GateMet still gates for real when critical genes are present). The other 3 modules are UNTOUCHED by M24 (clerkenstein 223 · stack-seeding 259 · stack-snapshot 316 — unchanged from M23 close). Method: Test+Fuzz func-decl count via grep -rohE '^func (Test|Fuzz)' per module.
tests.python_delta_note: M24 touched 1 python suite: stack-core 77->85 (+8) — the NEW corpus_index_guard.py's test_corpus_index_guard.py grew 8->16 across the build (8 initial tests) + harden Pass 1 (8 regression tests: prefix-collision, the five legitimate reference forms, dot-literal-not-wildcard, CORPUS_ROOT env fallback, non-UTF8 README doesn't crash, deterministic multi-orphan ordering, subdir-without-README, empty-README). The other 4 python suites (dev-stack, demo-stack, stack-injection, stack-verify) are untouched by M24. Counts via python3 -m unittest discover (stdlib unittest).
tests.test_count_method: Go: go test -count=1 ./... per module (fresh); func count via grep '^func (Test|Fuzz)'. Python: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' per touched module dir. 0 failures. The suites are stdlib unittest, not pytest — no junit XML emitted; the unittest 'Ran N tests ... OK' summary + exit code are the authority.
tests.lint: go vet + gofmt CLEAN on the M24-touched alignment packages (dna, compare); py_compile CLEAN on corpus_index_guard.py. The live corpus README-index guard itself runs exit 0 against corpus/ (every doc indexed, incl the M24-created directus-local.md + the 7 backfilled gaps).
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean on both M24-touched suites — Go alignment dna+compare run with -count=1 -shuffle=on (5/5 ok); python stack-core corpus_index_guard run sequentially 5x (5/5 ok). 0 failures across all runs. Note: pre-existing ResourceWarnings (unclosed files) appear in test_stack_registry.py — a pre-M24 file NOT touched by this milestone; warnings not failures, carried not fixed (a known pre-existing stack-core hygiene smell).

coverage.note: M24 ran a 2-pass hardening sweep (recorded inline in progress.md § Hardening). Pass 1 (the python corpus-index guard): statement coverage was already ~100% via stdlib trace, but the gaps were BEHAVIOURAL not line-based — Pass 1 surfaced + fixed the one real bug (the prefix-collision false-negative) + added 8 regression tests. Pass 2 (the Go zero-critical guard): dna 98.5% -> 100.0% statements; compare 91.1% steady (its residual uncovered lines are pre-existing non-M24 paths outside the diff); the M24-touched compare code (GateMet, CriticalGenes) is fully exercised, +3 dedicated tests. The Go pin (§4) is config (nothing to test); the /project-stats fix (§7) is a small cross-repo shell script left as-is.
coverage.harden_bugs_note: 1 real bug fixed inline in harden Pass 1: corpus_index_guard.py's raw-substring reference test had a PREFIX-COLLISION FALSE-NEGATIVE — an unindexed setup.md slipped through whenever a different, indexed dev-setup.md was present (setup.md is a substring of dev-setup.md), i.e. exactly the failure class the guard exists to catch. Fixed with a token-bounded regex match (basename preceded by a non-filename-continuation char, regex-escaped so dots are literal). Live corpus still exit 0. Commit 191d650 (ext). The close adversarial pass (Phase 2c) recorded 1 by-design scenario (incidental-mention lenient-by-filename) with 0 code change.

benchmarks.note: No performance-sensitive code in M24 (doc edits + a small per-directory README lint + a two-branch DNA-validation guard + go.mod toolchain pins). No threshold tests warranted.

review.findings_note: 5 findings: 0 scope · 2 code-quality (0 must-fix · 1 should-fix the non-UTF8 README stderr warning · 1 nice-to-have the two-layer guard comment clarity) · 0 docs · 0 tests · 1 adversarial-record (the README-index guard's incidental-mention lenient-by-filename, by design no fix) · 3 decision-triage (M24-D2 blend tag; M24-D1/-D3 archive). All addressed Fate-1; the 2 code-quality fixes committed in the ext repo (6a4749d).
review.scope: GREEN — all 7 sections checked; the overview In: list fully delivered Fate-1 (3 rosetta doc sections + 4 ext/dk hygiene items). No silently-dropped scope, no unaccounted TODO/FIXME. The Out items (M21-M23 mechanics + the DROPPED v1.5 features: AI content, shareability, more mirrors, deploy-CI gate, dev-up pre-warm) confirmed out. §7 landed cross-repo at its real source (developer-kit 825cdce, already committed) — not part of the rosetta merge.
review.code_quality: GREEN — 0 must-fix. The cross-cutting Explore review found the Python guard follows stack-core conventions (stdlib unittest, Path API, type hints, exit codes 0/1/2) with correct boundary safety (re.escape for dot-literal, token-bounded prefix match, non-UTF8 errors=replace, empty/missing-README handled, no leaked handles via read_text) and the Go changes follow alignment conventions (error-slice Validate, clean dna<->compare defence-in-depth at two layers, no dead code). 1 should-fix (non-UTF8 observability) + 1 nice-to-have (comment clarity) landed at close. go vet + gofmt + py_compile CLEAN.
review.adversarial: Phase 2c — 1 scenario probed live: does an INCIDENTAL filename mention (inside a URL / code fence / prose that isn't an index row) make the README-index guard treat an unindexed doc as 'referenced'? Yes (config.md inside a URL yields exit 0) — but this is the guard's DELIBERATE lenient-by-filename scope (catch the recurring real miss — a doc with NO mention — without policing where the mention sits, robust across the corpus's heterogeneous README styles). The complementary risk (a longer filename's tail masquerading) IS guarded (the token-bounded match, the real bug fixed in harden Pass 1). Recorded in decisions.md § Adversarial review; 0 code change.
review.docs: GREEN — the corpus truth-up verified by an independent Explore scan: ZERO remaining stale local-Directus claims (the fictional image tag / admin-password creds / compose snippet all gone + explicitly marked false), the --local-content flag + DEMO_NO_LOCAL_CONTENT env consistent across skills<->docs, M21-M23 in past tense, all relative md cross-references resolve, the live README-index guard exit 0. The 1 decision-blend (M24-D2) tagged into alignment_testing.md at close (mechanism text already accurate).
review.tests: GREEN — both M24-touched suites green (Go alignment dna+compare all pass, vet+gofmt clean; python stack-core 85 incl corpus_index_guard 16); coverage deepened by the 2-pass harden (dna -> 100%; the guard's behavioural gaps closed + the prefix-collision bug fixed); post-close flake gate 5/5 on both. No handbook test-count drift (no per-unit handbook quotes a count for the touched modules; stack-core/README.md quotes none).
review.decision_triage: M24-D2 (zero-critical guard, defence-in-depth at Validate+GateMet) BLENDED tag (#M24-D2) into corpus/architecture/alignment_testing.md § the zero-critical-genes guard (mechanism text was already accurate from build). M24-D1 (the Go toolchain directive) -> ARCHIVE (maintainer-only internal tooling-build detail; the release-level advisory status lives in state.md's Test-health line, not the platform corpus). M24-D3 (/project-stats scope fix + the surfaced stats.sh doc-counter limitation) -> ARCHIVE (cross-repo tooling detail; the observation IS its own audit trail in decisions.md). The full options-considered lists stay in decisions.md.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-13-m24-close.md. M24 introduced 0 new deferrals (all 4 chartered hygiene items NEW-5/6/11/14 landed Fate-1 — the milestone's job was to CLEAR aged-out hygiene backlog, and it did). 3 standing inherited items unchanged + not aged: DEF-M21-01 (replayCmd conn-seam, tracked follow-up) · DEF-M21-02 (serve-live-integration harness -> M25 field-bake) · DEF-M10-01 (S3 blob bytes, keep-deferred backlog). Verified M24's ext diff touches alignment + stack-core + go.mod pins only — NOT replay.go (DEF-M21-01's seam) nor the serve-row render path (DEF-M21-02), so no area-touched aging trigger fires. 0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out, 0 blockers.

The 4 chartered hygiene items (the aged-out backlog NEW-5/6/11/14) RESOLVED Fate-1 here.
completeness_ledger_section.confirmed_covered_fate2: none — M24 routed nothing forward of its own. (The 3 standing inherited deferrals are unchanged, already owned: DEF-M21-02 by M25; DEF-M21-01 by tracked tooling-debt; DEF-M10-01 by release-level backlog. None re-fated at M24.)
completeness_ledger_section.annotated_fate3: none — no new Fate-3 roadmap mutation introduced at M24 close.
completeness_ledger_section.escape_hatch_release_scope_breaking: none introduced by M24. DEF-M10-01 (cloud snapshot store + S3 media blob bytes) is the one INHERITED cross-release backlog item, untouched by M24 (no asset-plane code) — not a new escape-hatch.
completeness_ledger_section.observation_not_a_deferral: M24-D3 surfaced a pre-existing developer-kit stats.sh doc-counter limitation (it only scans knowledge/, so rosetta's corpus/ docs report 0; the eval-find PRUNE is shell-fragile) — independent of the §7 stack-*/ scanning bug (which WAS fixed). Explicitly NOT a v1.5 deliverable and NOT an M24 deferral (M24 §7 landed in full); recorded so the foreign-tool limitation isn't silently lost.

delivered.corpus_docs: rosetta corpus-wide truth-up: corpus/architecture/external_services.md (the stale local-Directus claims corrected — image/creds/compose-snippet marked false, the prod-read default + the v1.5 --local-content local tooling documented, Development Access + Troubleshooting bifurcated) · corpus/architecture/service_taxonomy.md (the Directus table -> prod-read default, no local dockerized service) · corpus/ops/quick_ops.md (the ports table no longer claims a local Directus always on 8055) · corpus/ops/directus-local.md (finished on the M23 data-plane cutover + referential closure, M23 past-tense) · corpus/ops/snapshot-spec.md (the known-state block leads with --local-content self-contained as the converged end-state, prod-read/exit-4 the fallback) · the corpus-wide language sweep across 5 skills (dev-up SKILL+reference, demo-up, stack-snapshot, db-query) + CLAUDE.md + 5 demo-corpus docs (demo/README + 3 recipes + seeding-spec) · the 7 backfilled README index gaps (5 architecture + 2 ops) so the new guard passes clean · the #M24-D2 blend tag in alignment_testing.md.
delivered.hygiene_strand: rosetta-extensions (tag prop-room-m24): (a) the Go toolchain pin — explicit toolchain go1.25.11 on all 4 go.mod (alignment, clerkenstein, stack-snapshot, stack-seeding) + the clerkenstein CI setup-go 1.25->1.25.11 (423bac7); (b) the README index-row guard — stack-core/corpus_index_guard.py, a token-bounded per-directory README index lint, 16 tests (d6dd8fc + the prefix-collision fix 191d650 + the close non-UTF8 warning); (c) the zero-critical-genes guard — alignment compare.go (CriticalGenes count + GateMet refuses a vacuous critical gate) + dna.go (Validate rejects a zero-critical DNA), defence-in-depth at two layers (04de89e + harden d01a3ee + the close comment clarity). PLUS (d) cross-repo: the /project-stats scope fix in the developer-kit plugin stats.sh — */stack-*/* in PRUNE_PATHS + a general drop_gitignored filter so the gitignored stack-*/ platform clones (~2M foreign lines / 9,235 files) are no longer scanned (ant-singularity 825cdce).

notes: M24 is the 4th milestone of v1.5 'prop room' — the docs-convergence + hygiene milestone that makes the corpus tell the new truth after the M21-M23 cutover and absorbs the 4 aged-out hygiene items the deferral audit surfaced. The load-bearing correction: the corpus carried a FICTIONAL local-Directus docker service (image directus/directus:10.10.1 + admin/password + a compose snippet) that NEVER existed in the platform compose — verified against stack-dev/platform/docker-compose.yml (no directus service, only the DIRECTUS_BASE_ADDR prod pointer). M24 corrects it + documents the real two-path posture (prod-read default; per-stack local Directus on --local-content). The README-index guard dog-fooded itself (surfaced 7 pre-existing gaps, all backfilled) and its harden surfaced the milestone's 1 real bug (a prefix-collision false-negative — the exact miss class the guard exists to catch). Close was clean: 5 findings (0 scope / 0 must-fix code-quality / 0 docs / 0 tests / 1 adversarial-record / 3 decision-triage), the deferral audit GREEN (0 new deferrals, the 4 chartered hygiene items all Fate-1, the 3 standing inherited items unchanged + not aged). Go 844 -> 850 (+6, all alignment — the zero-critical guard); python stack-core 77 -> 85 (+8, the corpus-index guard); flake 0 (5/5). Next: M25 (/developer-kit:build-milestone — the field-bake observable-behavior gate, the final v1.5 milestone).
